hero.title: Automated
hero.titleEnd: — Find Slots in Days, Not Months
hero.description: The #1 NEXUS appointment tracker and Global Entry slot finder. Our Chrome extension monitors interview cancellations 24/7 and sends instant alerts when appointments open up at your preferred enrollment centers.
hero.compatibility: Works on Chrome, Edge, and Brave

problem.heading: Appointment slots disappear in minutes
problem.description: NEXUS and Global Entry slots are released when someone cancels. They show up randomly and get booked almost instantly. Refreshing the website over and over is exhausting — and you still miss most of them.

fast.title: Slots vanish fast
fast.description: Cancelled appointments get snapped up within minutes. If you're not looking at the exact right moment, you lose it.

manual.title: Manual checking is painful
manual.description: There's no official notification system. You have to keep refreshing the GOES website yourself, hoping to get lucky.

wait.title: Wait times are months
wait.description: The next available slot might be 3-6 months away. But cancellations create openings much sooner — if you catch them.

pick.title: Pick your locations
pick.description: Select which enrollment centers you want to monitor. Choose NEXUS, Global Entry, or SENTRI.

set.title: Set your preferences
set.description: Filter by date range and time of day. Set how often to check — every 1, 3, 5, or 10 minutes.

notify.title: Get notified instantly
notify.description: When a slot opens, you get a desktop notification with a sound alert. One click takes you straight to booking.

features.heading: Everything you need
features.subheading: Built to make sure you never miss another opening.

monitoring.title: Real-time monitoring
monitoring.description: Checks for new slots as often as every minute, running silently in the background while you work.

sound.title: Sound alerts
sound.description: Hear a chime the moment a slot appears. No need to keep watching — just listen for the notification.

multiLocation.title: Multi-location tracking
multiLocation.description: Monitor multiple enrollment centers at once. Increases your chances of finding an opening quickly.

filters.title: Smart filters
filters.description: Only get alerts for slots that match your schedule. Filter by date range and time of day.

history.title: Slot history
history.description: See a log of every slot that's opened recently. Helps you learn the best times to check.

privacy.title: Privacy-first
privacy.description: Runs locally on your computer. No tracking, no data collection, no account required.

pricing.heading: Simple, transparent pricing
pricing.subheading: Start free, upgrade when you need more power

howWork.question: How does NEXUS Alert work?
howWork.answer: NEXUS Alert is a Chrome extension that automatically checks the CBP GOES website for appointment cancellations. When a slot opens at one of your preferred locations, you get an instant notification.

free.question: Is it really free?
free.answer: Yes! The free version includes all core features: monitoring up to 3 locations with checks every 5-10 minutes. Upgrade to Pro for faster checks and unlimited locations.

safe.question: Is it safe to use?
safe.answer: Absolutely. NEXUS Alert runs locally on your computer and doesn't collect or store any personal data. We never ask for your GOES login credentials.

programs.question: Which programs does it support?
programs.answer: NEXUS Alert works with NEXUS, Global Entry, and SENTRI appointment systems.

cancel.question: Can I cancel my Pro subscription?
cancel.answer: Yes, you can cancel anytime. If you cancel, you'll keep Pro features until the end of your billing period.

browsers.question: Which browsers are supported?
browsers.answer: NEXUS Alert works on Chrome, Microsoft Edge, and Brave browser.

cta.heading: Ready to find your appointment?
cta.description: Join thousands of users who have already booked their NEXUS or Global Entry appointments faster.

footer.disclaimer: NEXUS Alert is not affiliated with U.S. Customs and Border Protection (CBP) or the Department of Homeland Security (DHS).
